1: hinnēh mah-ṭôb ûmah-nāʿîm šebet ʾaḥîm gam-yaḥad — "how good and how pleasant it is when brothers dwell together as one!" — hinnēh (behold, look!) — the exclamation points toward a visible reality. mah-ṭôb and mah-nāʿîm (how good! how pleasant!) — two different qualities of goodness: ṭôb (the basic goodness of creation, Genesis 1:31) and nāʿîm (pleasantness, delight, aesthetic loveliness — the same root as Naomi). šebet ʾaḥîm gam-yaḥad (the sitting/dwelling of brothers also together) — gam-yaḥad (even/also together) — the gam (even, also) adds emphasis: brothers who are also one, not merely associated. The setting is the pilgrimage festival — the tribes assembled at Zion are "brothers" in the literal sense (one people) and in the symbolic sense (co-worshippers).
2: kaššemen haṭṭôb ʿal-hārōʾš yōrēd ʿal-hazzāqān zĕqan-ʾahărōn šeyōrēd ʿal-pî middôtāyw — "it is like costly anointing oil poured on the head, running down over Aaron's beard — flowing down to the edge of his robes" — šemen haṭṭôb (the good/precious oil) — the consecration oil of Exodus 30:22-25 (myrrh, cinnamon, cane, cassia in olive oil) used at Aaron's anointing (Leviticus 8:12). The oil flows from the head down — unity flows from the top of the community downward, anointing the whole. pî middôtāyw (lit. "the mouth/opening of his garments" = the collar/hem of his robes) — the oil reaches to the very edge of the priestly garment. The anointing that consecrates the High Priest covers him completely — so unity covers the whole community.
3: kĕṭal-ḥermôn šeyyōrēd ʿal-harrê ṣiyyôn kî šām ṣiwwāh yhwh ʾet-habbĕrākāh ḥayyîm ʿad-hāʿôlām — "it is like the dew of Hermon descending on the mountains of Zion — for there the LORD has commanded the blessing: life that lasts forever" — ṭal-ḥermôn (dew of Hermon) — Mount Hermon (~2,814m) in the far north collects enormous quantities of moisture; its dew was proverbial for abundance and refreshment. Geographically, Hermon's dew cannot literally fall on Zion (300km south) — the simile is about the quality and abundance of refreshment, not geographic precision. kî šām ṣiwwāh yhwh ʾet-habbĕrākāh — "for there (šām, emphatic) YHWH commanded the blessing" — the place of unity is where divine blessing is decreed. ḥayyîm ʿad-hāʿôlām (life unto the age/forever) — the blessing is life itself, permanent life. The Songs of Ascents culminate in this vision: the pilgrims gathered at Zion in unity receive the blessing of life that endures.
